seo.title: Government Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in government. Covers FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for data extraction in government contexts carries critical risk exposure—from national security breaches to civil liberties violations—making rigorous pre-deployment compliance essential. Federal mandates including FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 impose strict controls on how AI systems process, store, and transmit government data, particularly when extracting structured information from unstructured documents. This checklist ensures your data extraction pipeline meets federal standards, maintains explainability for citizen-facing decisions, and prevents classified or sensitive data from reaching unauthorized external systems.

sections.heading: Data Classification & Handling Controls
sections.description: Before any document enters the extraction pipeline, its classification level and handling requirements must be verified to prevent unauthorized disclosure or processing of controlled information.

items.label: Classify all input documents before ingestion
items.description: Implement automated and manual classification tagging (Unclassified, CUI, Secret, Top Secret) on every document before it enters the extraction pipeline. Reject or quarantine any document whose classification cannot be confirmed.

items.label: Block classified data from external LLM endpoints
items.description: Enforce a hard technical control—via network policy, data loss prevention (DLP) tooling, or API gateway rules—that prevents any document classified above Unclassified/CUI from being transmitted to external or commercial LLM APIs.

items.label: Validate CUI category markings against NARA CUI Registry
items.description: For Controlled Unclassified Information, verify that the specific CUI category (e.g., Privacy, Law Enforcement, Export Control) is identified and that handling rules for that category are enforced throughout the extraction workflow.

items.label: Implement data residency enforcement for extraction outputs
items.description: Ensure all extracted structured data is stored only in FedRAMP-authorized environments within approved geographic boundaries. Document the data flow from ingestion through extraction output storage.

items.label: Sanitize PII fields before logging or debugging
items.description: Configure the extraction pipeline to redact or tokenize PII fields (SSN, DOB, address) in all debug logs, audit trails, and error outputs to prevent incidental exposure during troubleshooting.

items.label: Define and enforce data retention schedules on extracted outputs
items.description: Apply NARA-compliant records retention schedules to extracted structured data. Automate deletion or archival of extraction outputs at the end of their authorized retention period.

items.label: Document data lineage from source document to extracted field
items.description: Maintain a traceable record linking each extracted field value back to its source document, page, and text span. This lineage is required for audit, appeal, and oversight purposes.

sections.heading: Schema Validation & Extraction Accuracy
sections.description: Government data extraction must produce structured outputs that are schema-compliant, type-consistent, and verifiably accurate—errors in extracted fields can directly affect citizen benefits, legal records, or national security assessments.

items.label: Define and version-control the extraction schema
items.description: Maintain a formally versioned JSON Schema or equivalent specification for every extraction target. Changes to the schema must go through a change control process and be backward-compatible or explicitly versioned.

items.label: Enforce strict type validation on all extracted fields
items.description: After each LLM extraction call, run automated type-checking against the schema (e.g., date fields parse as ISO 8601, numeric fields are within valid ranges, enum fields match allowed values). Reject outputs that fail type validation.

items.label: Implement completeness checks for required fields
items.description: Flag any extraction output where required schema fields are null, missing, or contain placeholder values. Route incomplete extractions to human review rather than allowing them to propagate downstream.

items.label: Run confidence scoring on extracted field values
items.description: Require the LLM to output a confidence score or evidence span for each extracted field. Establish agency-specific confidence thresholds below which human review is mandatory before the output is accepted.

items.label: Validate extracted values against authoritative reference data
items.description: Cross-reference extracted fields (e.g., agency codes, state identifiers, contract numbers) against authoritative government reference datasets to catch hallucinated or incorrect values.

items.label: Test extraction accuracy against a labeled government document benchmark
items.description: Before production deployment, evaluate the extraction pipeline against a labeled test set of at least 200 representative government documents. Document precision, recall, and F1 scores per field type.

items.label: Implement schema drift detection for evolving document formats
items.description: Monitor extraction outputs over time for increases in validation failures or field-level error rates that may indicate the source document format has changed. Trigger schema review when error rates exceed defined thresholds.

items.label: Log all schema validation failures with source document reference
items.description: Persist a structured log entry for every validation failure, including the document ID, failed field, received value, and expected type/format. Use these logs for continuous improvement and audit evidence.

sections.heading: Explainability, Auditability & Citizen Rights
sections.description: Federal AI policy and agency-specific mandates require that AI-assisted decisions affecting citizens be explainable, auditable, and subject to appeal—the extraction pipeline must support these requirements by design.

items.label: Conduct an Algorithmic Impact Assessment (AIA) before deployment
items.description: Complete a formal AIA documenting the extraction system's purpose, affected populations, potential harms, bias risks, and mitigation measures. Obtain sign-off from the agency's AI governance body or Chief AI Officer before go-live.

items.label: Implement an explanation record for each extraction decision
items.description: For every extracted field that influences a citizen-facing decision, generate and store a human-readable explanation identifying the source text span, the extraction rationale, and any uncertainty flags.

items.label: Build an appeal and correction workflow for affected citizens
items.description: Establish a documented process by which citizens can challenge, correct, or request re-review of decisions made using AI-extracted data. Ensure the workflow is accessible per Section 508 requirements.

items.label: Maintain immutable audit logs of all extraction pipeline executions
items.description: Store tamper-evident logs of every pipeline run, including input document hash, model version, schema version, extracted output hash, validation results, and operator identity. Retain logs per FISMA and agency records schedules.

items.label: Disclose AI involvement in data processing to affected parties
items.description: Where legally required or agency policy mandates, notify individuals when AI-assisted data extraction has been used in processing their records or in decisions affecting them.

items.label: Ensure Section 508 compliance for all human review interfaces
items.description: Audit all operator-facing dashboards, review queues, and citizen-facing appeal portals for Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance before deployment.

items.label: Document model version and prompt version for each extraction run
items.description: Record the exact LLM model version, system prompt hash, and schema version used for each extraction job so that outputs can be reproduced or audited months or years later.

sections.heading: Infrastructure Security & FedRAMP/FISMA Compliance
sections.description: The infrastructure hosting the extraction pipeline must meet federal security baselines—unauthorized access to the pipeline or its outputs could expose sensitive government data and trigger national security or privacy incidents.

items.label: Deploy extraction pipeline exclusively on FedRAMP-authorized infrastructure
items.description: Verify that every component of the extraction pipeline—compute, storage, API gateway, logging—is hosted on a FedRAMP Moderate or High authorized cloud service. Document the authorization boundary and include the AI pipeline within the system's ATO scope.

items.label: Obtain or update Authority to Operate (ATO) to include AI extraction components
items.description: Work with the agency ISSO to ensure the AI extraction system is explicitly included in the system's ATO. New AI capabilities that materially change the system's risk posture require an ATO amendment or new assessment.

items.label: Implement NIST 800-53 AC controls for extraction pipeline access
items.description: Enforce least-privilege access controls (AC-2, AC-3, AC-6) on all pipeline components. Require multi-factor authentication for all operators and administrators. Conduct quarterly access reviews.

items.label: Encrypt all data in transit and at rest using FIPS 140-2 validated modules
items.description: Ensure all document transfers, API calls, and stored extraction outputs use FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules. Verify TLS 1.2+ is enforced on all endpoints and that storage encryption keys are managed via a FIPS-compliant KMS.

items.label: Integrate extraction pipeline events into agency SIEM for continuous monitoring
items.description: Forward pipeline logs, authentication events, validation failures, and anomalous extraction patterns to the agency's Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system. Define alerting rules for high-volume failures or unauthorized access attempts.

items.label: Conduct penetration testing on extraction API endpoints before go-live
items.description: Perform a NIST 800-115-aligned penetration test targeting the extraction pipeline's API endpoints, authentication mechanisms, and data storage. Remediate all critical and high findings before production deployment.

items.label: Establish an incident response plan specific to AI extraction failures
items.description: Document a playbook for extraction-specific incidents: data exfiltration via prompt injection, mass extraction errors affecting citizen records, and model output poisoning. Assign roles, escalation paths, and notification timelines per FISMA IR requirements.

items.label: Perform supply chain risk assessment on LLM provider and dependencies
items.description: Conduct a NIST 800-161-aligned supply chain risk assessment on the LLM vendor, any third-party extraction libraries, and cloud dependencies. Verify the vendor's FedRAMP authorization status and review their incident notification SLAs.

notes.note: Any AI extraction system processing federal data must operate within a FedRAMP-authorized boundary and be included in the agency's FISMA system inventory with an active ATO. The ATO must explicitly address the AI/ML components, their risk posture, and the controls implemented—generic ATOs that predate the AI deployment are insufficient and expose the agency to audit findings.

NIST 800-53 Rev 5 introduced AI-relevant controls including SA-11 (developer testing and evaluation), SI-7 (software and information integrity), and the new supply chain risk management family (SR). For LLM-based extraction, agencies must document how the model's outputs are integrity-checked (SI-7), how the model vendor is assessed (SR-3), and how extraction logic is tested prior to deployment (SA-11).

notes.regulation: Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI (EO 14110)
notes.note: EO 14110 requires federal agencies to designate a Chief AI Officer, inventory AI use cases, and conduct risk assessments for AI systems that impact rights or safety. A data extraction system used in benefits processing, law enforcement records, or immigration decisions qualifies as a rights-impacting AI use case and must be reported in the agency's AI use case inventory with documented risk mitigations.

notes.regulation: Privacy Act of 1974 & Section 208 of E-Government Act
notes.note: If the extraction pipeline processes records about individuals from a Privacy Act System of Records, a Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) and potentially a System of Records Notice (SORN) amendment are required before deployment. The PIA must specifically address how the AI extraction system accesses, processes, and retains personal information, and must be reviewed by the agency's Senior Agency Official for Privacy (SAOP).

cta.heading: Automate Your Government Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your government AI extraction pipeline against FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 controls—catching compliance gaps before they become congressional investigations.
